title: citrus assamensis
intro: Citrus assamensis, also known as the 'Wild Orange', is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to Assam and other parts of north-eastern India. This plant has been found to possess various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of limonoids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Citrus assamensis include its ability to aid in digestion, boost the immune system, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions.
